title: Best Rap Album: Grammy Winners & Nominees By Year
text: Total Music Awards maintains a comprehensive archive of Best Rap Album Grammy winners and nominees by year. The archive provides historical context for understanding the evolution of the award and the artists who have been recognized. For 2026, the nominees represent a diverse cross-section of hip-hop talent with Kendrick Lamar's GNX ultimately winning. The archive shows patterns in Grammy voting for Best Rap Album over the years, with established artists often favored over newcomers. The nominees for 2026 included Kendrick Lamar (GNX), Clipse (Let God Sort Em Out), Tyler, The Creator (CHROMAKOPIA), GloRilla (GLORIOUS), and JID (God Does Like Ugly). These five albums represented different approaches to rap music, from traditional lyricism to experimental production to commercial hip-hop.
